refinedev/refine#6360.body:
### Describe the bug

Right now, Supabase Realtime does not support multiple filters. If used, the live provider for supabase will send an invalid filter payload, causing the realtime request to fail.

### Steps To Reproduce

1. Create any realtime resource
2. Add more than 1 filter to the resource (I was using a useList hook with multiple objects in the filters option)
3. Watch websocket network requests, it will show an error.

### Expected behavior

I think we should either log a warning if we see multiple filters, or naively take the first one? 

### Packages

- supabase realtime v2.30.34 (not latest, but also not fixed in latest)
- @refinedev/supabase 5.9.4

### Additional Context

See here for Refine's implementation, where it is joining each filter with a comma. 

https://github.com/refinedev/refine/blame/8be95d808ae2f3f280ebd015bd95eb04238fe463/packages/supabase/src/liveProvider/index.ts#L67

See here for feature that is being discussed. No real activity it seems.

https://github.com/supabase/realtime/issues/486
https://github.com/supabase/realtime-js/issues/97
refinedev/refine#6360.title: [BUG] Multiple Filters not Supported by Supabase Realtime

I have recently discovered a vite configuration issue that I fixed that prevented sourcemaps for refine core from working (I have had a lot of issues before then). However, even with this, is seems at-least 1 code path while debugging skipped an if statement based on the if for no reason at all.

I am requesting the project consider disabling minify in builds so that pages can properly be debugged without needing to jump through hoops as building locally, creating a patch and use pnpm or patch-package is honestly a last resort type of PITA. Kudos

Disable minify in published builds

refinedev/refine#6651.body:
### Describe the bug

If a `LogicalFilter` with the operator `in` is used inside a `ConditionalFilter` `or` the array value is parsed incorrectly by the `generateFilter` Method. 

When passing a array outside of a `LogicalFilter` it parses correctly. 





### Steps To Reproduce

I am using the following Filter configuration: 
```javascript
{
        operator: 'or',
        value: [
        {
           field: "id",
           operator: 'in',
           value: [1,2,3],
        },
        ... more filters
    ],
},

```
This is sending the following to supabase as filter: 
`http://127.0.0.1:54321/rest/v1/company?select=*&offset=0&limit=30&or=(id.in."1","2","4")`

Supabase then throws the follwing error: 

```
{
    "code": "PGRST100",
    "details": "unexpected \"1\" expecting \"(\"",
    "hint": null,
    "message": "\"failed to parse logic tree ((id.in.1,2,4))\" (line 1, column 10)"
}
```

### Expected behavior

This is missing the parentheses around the array. It should look like this:
`http://127.0.0.1:54321/rest/v1/company?select=*&offset=0&limit=30&or=(id.in.("1","2","4"))
`
### Packages

 - @refinedev/supabase

### Additional Context

_No response_
refinedev/refine#6651.title: [BUG] [Supabase] When a "in" filter is nested it is parsed incorrectly

refinedev/refine#6713.body:
### Describe the bug

I'm using GraphQL and I'm changing the data with the React Query's `select` middleware. It should run only once per request, but it is running multiple times. 

### Steps To Reproduce

1. Use the `useList` hook
2. Add `queryOptions.select: (data) => { console.log('select'); return data }`
3. See "select" is logged multiple times

Replication CodeSandbox: https://codesandbox.io/p/sandbox/react-query-5-forked-hh85pv

### Expected behavior

It should run only once. In React Query's documentation, they say to memoize the select function: https://tanstack.com/query/latest/docs/framework/react/guides/render-optimizations#memoization

`useList` is changing the function reference on every render.

### Packages

- "@refinedev/core": "^4.54.0",

### Additional Context

Possible fix: pass the `select` function inside a `useCallback` to `useList`'s `useQuery`
refinedev/refine#6713.title: [BUG] `queryOptions.select` runs multiple times

### Describe the bug

When using the Hasura data provider, meta.gqlVariables is used for getOne, getMany, and getList, but not for any other queries. This prevents, for example, useForm's mutationMeta.gqlVariables from applying.

### Steps To Reproduce

1. Add gqlVariables object with variables defined inside to the meta field of any hook which uses create, update, or delete, alongside a gql operation (meta.gqlQuery or meta.gqlMutation)
2. Expectation: gqlVariables are passed to the gql operation, reality: they usually are not

### Expected behavior

when using a meta gql operation such as gqlQuery or gqlMutation, meta.gqlVariables should always be passed to the operation.

### Packages

- @refinedev/hasura

### Additional Context

_No response_

refinedev/refine#6833.body:
### Describe the bug

When using `useDataGrid` with `updateMutationOptions` and `editable: true`, the receiving hook does not get passed the metadata for determining the required GraphQL mutation when a cell/row update is performed.

### Steps To Reproduce

Data Grid:
```typescript
const { 
      dataGridProps,
     } = useDataGrid<IPost>({
        resource: "posts",
        meta: { 
            operation: "PostList",
            gqlQuery: POST_LIST, 
            gqlMutation: POST_UPDATE,
        },
        pagination: {mode: "client", pageSize:20},
        editable: true,
        updateMutationOptions: {
            meta:{
                gqlMutation: POST_UPDATE,
            },
            retry: 1,
        }
    });
```

With at least one editable column:
```typescript
            // ...
            {
                field: "name",
                headerName: "Name",
                type: "string",
                minWidth: 500,
                sortable: true,
                filterable: false,
                editable: true,
            }
```

### Expected behavior

Editable performs a call to `update` of the (GraphQL) data provider with `POST_UPDATE` as gqlOperation:

```typescript
      const { meta } = params;
      const gqlOperation = meta?.gqlMutation ?? meta?.gqlQuery;
```

### Packages

```
        "@refinedev/cli": "^2.16.21",
        "@refinedev/core": "^4.57.9",
        "@refinedev/devtools": "^1.1.32",
        "@refinedev/graphql": "^7.1.3",
        "@refinedev/inferencer": "^5.0.3",
        "@refinedev/kbar": "^1.3.6",
        "@refinedev/mui": "^6.0.0",
        "@refinedev/react-hook-form": "^4.8.14",
        "@refinedev/react-router": "^1.0.0",
```
refinedev/refine#6833.title: [BUG] useDataGrid + GraphQL: update does not receive meta.gqlMutation for editable cell

refinedev/refine#6878.body:
# 🚀 Refine v5: TanStack Query v5 + React 19 Support

**Related Issues:**

- https://github.com/refinedev/refine/issues/6574
- https://github.com/refinedev/refine/issues/5370

## Overview

Modernize Refine to support TanStack Query v5 and React 19 with comprehensive deprecation cleanup.

---

## TanStack Query v5 Migration

### Core Hook Updates

- [x] **Data Hooks:** `useList`, `useOne`, `useMany`, `useCustom`, `useInfiniteList`
- [x] **Mutation Hooks:** `useCreate`, `useUpdate`, `useDelete`, `useCreateMany`, `useUpdateMany`, `useDeleteMany`
- [x] **Auth Hooks:** `useLogin`, `useLogout`, `useRegister`, `useGetIdentity`, `usePermissions`

### Breaking Changes Handling

- [x] Fix callback patterns: `onSuccess`/`onError` is removed from TanStack Query v5
- [x] Update query signature: `useQuery(key, fn, options)` → `useQuery({ queryKey, queryFn, ...options })`
- [x] Add required `initialPageParam` to infinite queries
- [x] Add required `queryKey` and `queryFn` to all hooks
- [x] Update AutoSave indicator for new Tanstack Query v5 status API

---

## Core Package Deprecation Cleanup

### Legacy Resource Type Cleanup

- [x] Remove deprecated `options` property from ResourceProps → use `meta`
- [x] Remove deprecated `canDelete` property from ResourceProps → use `meta.canDelete`
- [x] Remove deprecated `icon` property from ResourceProps → use `meta.icon`
- [x] Remove deprecated `parentName` property from ResourceProps → use `meta.parent`
- [x] Remove deprecated `key` property from ResourceProps (not used anymore)
- [x] Remove deprecated `label` property from IResourceItem → use `meta.label`
- [x] Remove deprecated `route` property from IResourceItem
- [x] Remove deprecated `DeprecatedOptions` interface entirely
- [x] Remove deprecated `RouteableProperties` interface (canCreate, canEdit, canShow, canDelete)

### useResource Hook Modernization

- [x] Remove deprecated legacy props interface (`UseResourceLegacyProps`)
- [x] Remove deprecated return properties (`resourceName`, `id`, `action`)
- [x] Migrate ErrorComponent to use `useResourceParams` instead of deprecated `useResource().action`
- [x] Keep `resourceNameOrRouteName` support (still actively used in UI libraries)

### Core Helper Updates

- [x] Update `routeGenerator` to use `meta.route` instead of `options.route`
- [x] Update `generateDocumentTitle` to use only `meta.label`
- [x] Update `navigation-button` to remove `resource.label` fallback
- [x] Update `sanitize-resource` to handle only `meta` properties
- [x] Update `get-parent-resource` to use only `meta.parent`

### Test Suite Migration

- [x] Migrate all test files from deprecated props to `meta` object pattern
- [x] Update `useMenu.spec.tsx` with modern resource definitions
- [x] Remove deprecated `key`, `parentName`, `options`, `canDelete` usage in tests
- [x] Update test utilities to use `meta` instead of `options`

### Deprecated Module/Component Removal

- [x] **Component Deletions**

  - [x] Remove `layoutWrapper` component and tests entirely
  - [x] Remove `pages/login` component (deprecated LoginPage)
  - [x] Remove `pages/ready` component and tests (deprecated ReadyPage)
  - [x] Remove exports from components/index.ts and pages/index.ts

- [x] **Legacy Router Infrastructure**

  - [x] Remove `contexts/router/legacy/index.tsx` and types
  - [x] Remove `contexts/router/picker/index.tsx`
  - [x] Remove `hooks/legacy-router/index.ts` and `useRouterContext.ts`

- [x] **Helper Function Cleanup**
  - [x] Remove `definitions/helpers/legacy-resource-transform` and tests
  - [x] Remove `definitions/helpers/pickNotDeprecated` and tests
  - [x] Remove `definitions/helpers/queryKeys` and tests (replaced by TanStack Query v5)
  - [x] Remove `definitions/helpers/routeGenerator` and tests (completely removed)
  - [x] Remove `definitions/helpers/treeView` modules entirely
  - [x] Remove `definitions/helpers/useActiveAuthProvider` test file
  - [x] Remove `definitions/helpers/userFriendlyResourceName` and tests

### Legacy Parameter Removal

- [x] Remove `@deprecated metaData` → use `meta` (affects 15+ hooks)
- [x] Remove `@deprecated sort` → use `sorters` (affects useList, useTable, useSelect)
- [x] Clean up `pickNotDeprecated` function usage across codebase
- [x] Update interface definitions and type exports
- [x] Legacy Auth Provider infrastructure cleanup
- [x] Legacy Router infrastructure cleanup

---

## React 19 Support

### Package Dependencies

- [x] Update peer dependencies to support React 18/19: `"react": "^18.0.0 || ^19.0.0"`
- [x] Update TypeScript types: `@types/react`, `@types/react-dom`
- [ ] Test compatibility across all packages

---

## UI Package Cleanups

### 📦 Ant Design Package (`packages/antd`)

#### Button Components Modernization

- [x] Remove `resourceNameOrRouteName` prop from CloneButton
- [x] Remove `resourceNameOrRouteName` prop from EditButton
- [x] Remove `resourceNameOrRouteName` prop from ShowButton
- [x] Remove `resourceNameOrRouteName` prop from CreateButton
- [x] Remove `resourceNameOrRouteName` prop from ListButton
- [x] Remove `resourceNameOrRouteName` prop from DeleteButton
- [x] Remove `resourceNameOrRouteName` prop from RefreshButton
- [x] Remove `recordItemId` prop from all button components
- [x] Update button implementation to use only `resource` and `id` props

#### Hook Parameter Updates

- [x] Remove `metaData` parameter from useForm → use only `meta`
- [x] Remove `metaData` parameter from useTable → use only `meta`
- [x] Remove `metaData` parameter from useSimpleList → use only `meta`
- [x] Remove `metaData` parameter from useSelect → use only `meta`
- [x] Remove `metaData` parameter from useCheckboxGroup → use only `meta`
- [x] Remove `metaData` parameter from useRadioGroup → use only `meta`
- [x] Remove deprecated query parameters from field hooks
- [x] Clean up `pickNotDeprecated(meta, metaData)` patterns

#### Resource Property Cleanup

- [x] Remove deprecated `route` property access in edit component
- [x] Remove deprecated `route` property access in show component
- [x] Remove deprecated `route` property access in list component
- [x] Update IMenuItem interface to use `meta.route` instead of `route`
- [x] Clean up legacy route fallback patterns in CRUD components

#### Test Suite Updates

- [x] Replace `canDelete: true/false` with `meta: { canDelete: true/false }` in edit tests
- [x] Replace `canDelete: true/false` with `meta: { canDelete: true/false }` in show tests
- [x] Update all CRUD component tests (edit, show, list)
- [x] Remove deprecated resource property usage in test fixtures

#### Sider Component Updates

- [x] Remove `pickNotDeprecated(meta?.parent, options?.parent, parentName)` from themedLayoutV2 sider
- [x] Remove `pickNotDeprecated(meta?.parent, options?.parent, parentName)` from themedLayout sider
- [x] Remove `pickNotDeprecated(meta?.parent, options?.parent, parentName)` from layout sider
- [x] Use only `meta.parent` for resource hierarchy

#### Deprecated Component Cleanup

- [x] Remove or update LoginPage (marked deprecated)
- [x] Remove or update ReadyPage (marked deprecated)
- [x] Remove or update WelcomePage (marked deprecated)
- [x] Remove or update Layout (marked deprecated)
- [x] Remove or update ThemedLayout (marked deprecated)
- [x] Remove or update notificationProvider (marked deprecated)

### 📦 Material-UI Package (`packages/mui`)

- [x] Apply same button component updates as antd package
- [x] Apply same hook parameter updates as antd package
- [x] Apply same resource property cleanup as antd package
- [x] Apply same test suite updates as antd package
- [x] Apply same sider component updates as antd package
- [x] Apply same deprecated component cleanup as antd package

### 📦 Mantine Package (`packages/mantine`)

- [x] Apply same button component updates as antd package
- [x] Apply same hook parameter updates as antd package

refinedev/refine#6983.body: ### Describe the bug When using errorNotification as a function in any Refine hooks (including useShow, useTable, useList, etc.), the error notification triggers repeatedly in an infinite loop when an error occurs. This appears to be caused by Refine's internal useEffect implementation where the notification function is re-executed on every render due to dependency changes. The issue occurs consistently across different Refine hooks when errorNotification is defined as a function that returns notification configuration, rather than a static object. ### Steps To Reproduce 1. Use any Refine hook (useShow, useTable, useList, etc.) with errorNotification as a function 2. Define the function to return notification configuration object:// Example with useShow const { query } = useShow({ resource: 'some-resource', errorNotification: error => ({ message: 'An error occurred, please try again later', description: 'Please contact administrator for support', type: 'error', }), }); 3. Trigger an API error (e.g., network failure, 404, 500 error) 4. Observe that the error notification appears multiple times in a continuous loop ### Expected behavior The error notification should only be displayed once when an error occurs, regardless of which Refine hook is used. The notification function should be called only when there's an actual error, not on every re-render or useEffect execution. ### Packages "@refinedev/antd": "^6.0.1", "@refinedev/cli": "^2.16.48", "@refinedev/core": "^5.0.0", "@refinedev/devtools": "^2.0.1", "@refinedev/react-router": "^2.0.0", ### Additional Context This issue affects multiple Refine hooks including but not limited to: useShow useTable useList useOne useMany The problem appears to be in Refine's internal notification handling mechanism that uses useEffect without proper dependency management for function-based errorNotification callbacks.
refinedev/refine#6983.title: [BUG] errorNotification function causes infinite notification loop in Refine hooks

refinedev/refine#7013.body:
### Describe the bug

After PR #6948 (refactor: pagination `current` -> currentPage), the `syncWithLocation` feature is broken when using Next.js App Router. The `current` parameter from URL is not being passed to `useTable`, causing pagination to always default to page 1.

### Steps To Reproduce

1. Set up Refine with Next.js App Router
2. Use `useTable` with `syncWithLocation: true`
3. Navigate to URL with `?current=2&pageSize=10`
4. Observe that the table shows page 1 data instead of page 2

### Expected behavior

- URL parameter `?current=2` should load page 2 data
- Table pagination should show page 2 as active
- `syncWithLocation` should work automatically without manual intervention

### Packages

- **@refinedev/core**: 5.0.1
- **@refinedev/nextjs-router**: 7.0.0
- **@refinedev/antd**: 6.0.1
- **Next.js**: 15.2.4
- **React**: 19.1.0

### Additional Context

## Describe the bug

After PR #6948 (refactor: pagination `current` -> currentPage), the `syncWithLocation` feature is broken when using Next.js App Router. The `current` parameter from URL is not being passed to `useTable`, causing pagination to always default to page 1.

## Steps To Reproduce

1. Set up Refine with Next.js App Router
2. Use `useTable` with `syncWithLocation: true`
3. Navigate to URL with `?current=2&pageSize=10`
4. Observe that the table shows page 1 data instead of page 2

## Expected behavior

- URL parameter `?current=2` should load page 2 data
- Table pagination should show page 2 as active
- `syncWithLocation` should work automatically without manual intervention

## Actual behavior

- URL shows `?current=2` but table loads page 1 data
- `parsedParams.params.current` is `undefined`
- `parsedParams.params.currentPage` contains the correct value ('2')
- `useTable` still looks for `current` parameter (line 242 in useTable/index.ts)

## Root Cause Analysis

The issue appears to be in the refactoring of PR #6948:

1. **routerProvider** (`@refinedev/nextjs-router/src/app/bindings.tsx` lines 128-130) still processes `current` parameter
2. **But** it stores the value in `currentPage` field instead of `current` field
3. **useTable** (`@refinedev/core/src/hooks/useTable/index.ts` line 242) still looks for `parsedParams?.params?.current`
4. **Result**: `current` is always `undefined`, causing pagination to default to page 1

## Debug Output

```javascript
// URL: ?current=2&pageSize=10
// parsedParams result:
{
  params: {
    pageSize: 10, 
    currentPage: '2',    // ✅ Value is here
    current: undefined,  // ❌ But useTable looks for this
    to: undefined
  }
}
```

## Environment

- **@refinedev/core**: 5.0.1
- **@refinedev/nextjs-router**: 7.0.0
- **@refinedev/antd**: 6.0.1
- **Next.js**: 15.2.4
- **React**: 19.1.0

## Temporary Workaround

```typescript
// Manually read currentPage from parsedParams
const parsedParams = useParsed();
const currentPageFromParams = parseInt(parsedParams?.params?.currentPage || '1', 10);

const { tableProps } = useTable({
  syncWithLocation: true,
  pagination: {
    currentPage: currentPageFromParams, // Manual fix
    pageSize: 2,
    mode: 'server',
  },
});
```

## Suggested Fix

Either:
1. Update routerProvider to set `current` field instead of `currentPage`
2. Or update useTable to look for `currentPage` instead of `current`
3. Or ensure backward compatibility by setting both fields

## Related Issues

This might be related to the incomplete refactoring in PR #6948 where some parts were changed from `current` to `currentPage` but the integration between routerProvider and useTable was not fully synchronized.

### Describe the bug

 
When `<NavigateToResource />` cannot find a resource, it only shows a warning.  
There is no way to provide a custom path for redirection.



### Steps To Reproduce

1. Remove `resources` array in `<Refine />`
2. Add `<NavigateToResource />` to any page
3. Go to any page

### Expected behavior

It should be possible to define a fallback path so the user can be redirected to a specific page if the resource is not found.

### Example
```tsx
<NavigateToResource resource="posts" fallbackTo="/dashboard" />
```

With this approach, if the `resource` is not available, the user will be redirected to `/dashboard` (or any custom path provided).

Add a refine config option that will make `RouteChangeHandler` in the Refine container opt-out.

I have been using it, but the v5 upgrade has caused it to change behaviors as currently in my app it loads such the default object is setup for the context and it return false, but the checking is run outside of the Router context for me due to architectural reasons. _No response_

refinedev/refine#7343.body:
### Describe the bug

When `hideText=true` is passed to `ShowButton`, the button body always renders
`<VisibilityOutlined />` regardless of any custom `startIcon` prop provided by the user.
This causes two icons to appear simultaneously — the custom icon (rendered as `startIcon`)
and the hardcoded `VisibilityOutlined` (rendered as children).

### Steps To Reproduce

```tsx
  <ShowButton
    recordItemId={1}
    hideText
    startIcon={<InsertDriveFileIcon />}
  />
```

Both InsertDriveFileIcon (as startIcon) and the default icon VisibilityOutlined (as children) are displayed.

### Expected behavior

Only InsertDriveFileIcon is displayed.

### Packages

 - @refinedev/cli: 2.16.49
 - @refinedev/core: 4.58.0
 - @refinedev/devtools: 1.2.17
 - @refinedev/inferencer: 4.7.1
 - @refinedev/kbar: 1.3.17
 - @refinedev/mui: 5.22.0
 - @refinedev/nextjs-router: 6.2.3
 - @refinedev/react-hook-form: 4.10.2
 - @refinedev/simple-rest: 5.0.11
 - @refinedev/supabase: 5.9.8

### Additional Context

#### Root Cause

  In the current implementation, when hideText=true, the button children are hardcoded
  to <VisibilityOutlined fontSize="small" {...svgIconProps} /> and the children prop
  passed by the user is ignored:

```tsx
  {hideText ? (
    <VisibilityOutlined fontSize="small" {...svgIconProps} />
  ) : (
    children ?? label
  )}
```

 #### Suggested Fix

  Use the startIcon prop (if provided) as the icon instead of always falling back to
  VisibilityOutlined:

```tsx
  const resolvedIcon = startIcon ?? <VisibilityOutlined fontSize="small" {...svgIconProps} />;

  {hideText ? resolvedIcon : children ?? label}
```
refinedev/refine#7343.title: [BUG] ShowButton ignores custom `startIcon` when `hideText=true`, causing double icon display
